title: Photo scroll: Scenes from Boston’s ‘No Kings’ protest on March 28
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Thousands gathered for the 'No Kings' protest on Boston Common on March 28, 2026.
'No Kings' protest events took place across Massachusetts, totaling over 160 gatherings.
Participants displayed signs with messages like 'No Kings Then 1776; No Kings Now 2026'.

title: Photos: ‘No Kings’ sparks protests in Europe
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Protests occur in Europe, including Madrid and Paris, as part of the 'No Kings' movement against Donald Trump.
Over 3,000 'No Kings' protests are planned across the U.S., with Massachusetts hosting more than 160 events.
Demonstrations in Rome and Amsterdam focus on issues like the Middle East war, aligning with 'No Kings' themes.

title: Bravo Just Dropped the first full “Real Housewives of Rhode Island” Trailer. Here’s everything we learned.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: 'The Real Housewives of Rhode Island' premieres April 2 at 9 p.m. on Bravo.
The show features Alicia Carmody, a Providence resident, and Liz McGraw, known as the state's 'Cannabis Queen.'
The series explores Rhode Island's tightly woven social scene with deep community roots and shifting loyalties.

title: Hampden County Sheriff plans dozens of job cuts
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Hampden County Sheriff's Department plans to cut about 50 jobs to save $4 million following a $26.5 million budget reduction.
The cuts will affect police presence in Springfield, West Springfield, Holyoke, and Palmer, with reduced patrols and response times.
Sheriff Nick Cocchi attributes the budget shortfalls to structural funding issues stemming from legislative practices after 1999.

title: Quincy approves money for new firefighting gear after PFAS found in old gear
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy approved $2.65 million to replace firefighters' gear containing cancer-linked PFAS with safer alternatives.
Firefighters have worn gear with PFAS for six years; new gear aims to protect 270 staff members.
Massachusetts will ban the sale of gear with added PFAS chemicals starting January 2025.

title: With his new doc  ‘Henry David Thoreau,’ Don Henley returns to Walden
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Don Henley discusses new documentary 'Henry David Thoreau' at Concord Carlisle Regional High School.
The Eagles co-founder founded The Walden Woods Project in 1990 to preserve Thoreau's historic grounds.
Narrated by George Clooney, the documentary airs on PBS March 30 and 31, featuring notable voice actors.

title: World Cup friendly match at Gillette was an ‘amazing’ off-day field trip for the Celtics
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Celtics attended a World Cup friendly match between France and Brazil at Gillette Stadium.
Kylian Mbappe scored for France, leading to a 2-1 victory over Brazil in the match.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla noted parallels between basketball and soccer, emphasizing the importance of possession.

title: Payton Pritchard’s huge night leads short-handed Celtics over streaking Hawks: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Payton Pritchard scored a game-high 36 points, leading the Celtics to a 109-102 victory over the Hawks.
Jayson Tatum contributed 25 points, 12 rebounds, and 5 assists, playing 36 minutes despite not fully finding his shooting rhythm.
Celtics coach Mazzulla confirmed Jaylen Brown is day-to-day with left Achilles tendinitis, missing the game against the Hawks.

title: Why did Jayson Tatum come back this season? The Celtics’ success had ‘a lot’ to do with it.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics this season, recovering from a ruptured Achilles' tendon in less than a year.
Tatum's decision to return was influenced by the Celtics' strong position and the opportunity to contend for a championship.
Since returning, Tatum averages 9.2 rebounds per game but struggles with shooting, posting 39% from the field.

title: Police: Man killed in Lowell apartment complex shooting
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A man was killed in a shooting at a Lowell apartment complex on Pawtucket Boulevard Friday afternoon.
The shooting resulted from an altercation between two men who were both allegedly armed, according to investigators.
The incident is under investigation by the Middlesex DA's Office, Massachusetts State Police, and Lowell Police Department.
